Analysis: Use a strong pretrained backbone (ConvNeXt-Tiny or ViT-CLIP), fine-tune on CIFAR-100-LT with class-balanced loss. First iteration: get baseline working with few epochs, submit eval to measure time.

trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline 0.8084 bal_acc with 5 epochs ConvNeXt-Tiny at 224. Many-shot 0.89, few-shot 0.71. Analysis: Few-shot classes underperform — need better imbalance handling. Strategies: (1) longer training, (2) two-stage training (CE then CB-loss/cRT), (3) MixUp/CutMix, (4) larger backbone like CLIP ViT-B/16 might help. Time: 80s for 5 epochs ≈ 16s/epoch. With ~2.9h budget, can afford ~30 epochs ConvNeXt or try CLIP. Decision: Try a stronger run — 20 epochs with mixup, two-stage approach: stage 1 standard CE for representations, stage 2 fine-tune classifier with class-balanced loss. Also use deferred re-weighting (DRW). Actually simpler: just train longer with better augmentation and DRW (apply class weights only after epoch 60% in).

trajectory.description: Observation: 20-epoch run with DRW + logit adjustment + mixup gave best val bal_acc 0.8340 (similar to baseline 0.808 eval). Training plateaued around epoch 9. Analysis: The combination may be too aggressive — logit adjustment + class weights compounded. Val accuracy on 1000 samples is noisy. The pipeline is working but improvements are marginal. Decision: Submit eval to see real test score, then try CLIP ViT-B/16 which is typically much stronger for transfer learning.

trajectory.description: Observation: 20-epoch ConvNeXt-Tiny with DRW + logit adjustment + mixup achieved 0.8351 test bal_acc (+0.027 over baseline). Many-shot 0.91, few-shot 0.76. Analysis: The training plateaued around epoch 9 — model converges quickly. Few-shot still lags. CLIP ViT-B/16 is typically stronger for transfer. Given ~2.9h remaining and 20-epoch ConvNeXt took ~250s, I have budget to try CLIP. Decision: Switch to CLIP ViT-B/16 backbone with same training setup. CLIP has better representations for transfer, often gives big gains on long-tail.

trajectory.description: Observation: CLIP ViT-B/16 with 12 epochs only got 0.769 val bal_acc — much worse than ConvNeXt's 0.834. Analysis: CLIP backbone fine-tuning at LR=5e-5 may need more epochs, lower LR for backbone, or be undertrained. Looking at the trend, it was still improving at epoch 11. Also LR is plateauing too fast. ConvNeXt converged faster and to higher value. Decision: Stick with ConvNeXt-Tiny (best so far 0.8351). Try to push it further: train longer with better hyperparameters, possibly use larger image resolution variants. Since ConvNeXt at 20 epochs achieved 0.8351, try 30 epochs with stronger augmentation (cutmix), and slightly different schedule. Best.pt is currently the ConvNeXt — don't overwrite until improvement confirmed.

trajectory.description: Observation: 30-epoch ConvNeXt got best val 0.841 at epoch 10, then DRW kicked in at epoch 18 and HURT performance (dropped to 0.78-0.83). The best model was actually pre-DRW. My current best.pt was overwritten with this run's epoch 10 (val 0.841) — actually need to check what was saved. The previous test eval was 0.8351 with the 20-epoch run. Analysis: DRW with strong class weights is hurting. Logit adjustment alone (already in loss) is doing the heavy lifting. Pre-DRW model at epoch 10 hit 0.841 val — promising. Decision: Drop DRW (or weaken it), train ~20 epochs without DRW, just use logit adjustment + mixup/cutmix. Also try CLIP ViT-B/16 with better hyperparameters separately. First check what's in checkpoints/best.pt.
